note: APPEND-ONLY store. The weekly job only ADDS items (autoDetected=true, use=''). It never edits or removes existing entries. Human-curated 'use' lines are added by editing this file directly and are never touched by automation.

issues.s: Temporary discontinuation while a new manufacturer awaits certification; delisted Jun/Jul 2026, resolution expected 01/12/2026.
issues.use: Trusts using BioFlo PICCs need an alternative until at least December 2026 — a live opening for other PICC suppliers.

issues.s: Discontinued through rationalisation; progressive delisting since Jan 2025, all codes delisted by 31/07/2026.
issues.use: Any trust still on Venflon must convert now — an immediate switching conversation for every cannula supplier.

issues.s: Global product removal for material-fatigue leak risk (FSN updated 15/05/2025); unused stock destroyed.
issues.use: Context for PICC conversations — ask what the trust moved to and whether the replacement is bedding in well.

issues.p: Cook FSN covering several sets, including the Spectrum Central Venous Catheter Set
issues.s: Field safety notice dated 05/03/2026 (MHRA ref 38698628) listing multiple Cook products including the Spectrum Central Venous Catheter Set; the MHRA listing does not state the nature of the issue — read the FSN itself before quoting it.
issues.use: A live Cook FSN touches the Spectrum central venous catheter set — ask your central line accounts whether they were affected and what action they took, without assuming the fault.

issues.s: Auto-detected from MHRA / GOV.UK - open the source for details and verify before use.

issues.p: Interventional urology portfolio (110 NHS Supply Chain lines)
issues.s: Field Safety Notice 16/12/2024: withdrawal over possible sterility issue; 42 lines suspended, 3 permanently deactivated.
issues.use: Ask which lines your accounts lost and whether replacements have fully restored service.

issues.s: Ongoing supply issue; seven codes still suspended as of May 2026; 12 PTFE-coated Foley lines discontinued and delisted.
issues.use: Affected trusts need alternative Foley supply now — a direct opening for other indwelling-catheter suppliers.

issues.s: Wellead range delisted from 01/05/2026; Folysil reinstated as the sole Coloplast silicone Foley option.
issues.use: Trusts that used Wellead have just changed product — worth asking how the transition has gone.

issues.s: SUSPECTED FALSE POSITIVE — no continence, urology or vascular access product on this page. Checked 20/07/2026; safe to delete.
SUSPECTED FALSE POSITIVE — likely matched on Siemens Atellica CH Urine Albumin, a laboratory IVD reagent, not a continence product. Checked 20/07/2026; safe to delete.
